tasks.title: Create a Flask app skeleton with a health-check route at GET /health
tasks.description: Set up a basic Flask application with a single route that returns a 200 OK status and a simple message.

tasks.title: Add a SQLite Expense model with fields: id, amount, category, note, created_at
tasks.description: Define an Expense model using SQLAlchemy ORM with the specified fields. Ensure the `created_at` field is automatically set to the current time when a new expense is added.
acceptance_criteria: The Expense table exists in the database with columns for id
and created_at. The created_at column is auto-filled with the current timestamp upon creation of a new record.

tasks.title: Add POST /expenses that creates an expense; reject amounts <= 0 with HTTP 400
tasks.description: Implement an endpoint that accepts JSON data to create a new expense. Validate the incoming data, especially the amount, which must be greater than 0. Return appropriate responses based on validation.
acceptance_criteria: Accepts valid JSON input and creates a new expense. Returns HTTP 201 on successful creation. Returns HTTP 400 if the amount is less than or equal to 0.

tasks.title: Add GET /expenses that returns all expenses as JSON, newest first
tasks.description: Create an endpoint that queries the database for all expenses, orders them by the `created_at` field in descending order, and returns the result as JSON.
acceptance_criteria: Returns a list of all expenses in JSON format
ordered from the most recent to the oldest. Returns an empty list if no expenses are found.

tasks.title: Add a pytest suite covering the create-and-list happy path and the negative-amount rejection
tasks.description: Write a series of pytest tests to verify the functionality of creating and listing expenses, including the handling of invalid (negative) amounts.
acceptance_criteria: Test suite includes tests for successfully creating an expense
and rejecting expenses with negative amounts. All tests pass.
